audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with lion/lamb throne imagery; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for sealed scroll, lion/lamb reversal, new song, priestly reign, universal worship.

verses.restored: And I saw upon the right hand of the One sitting upon the throne a scroll written within and on back, sealed with seven seals.
And I saw a strong messenger proclaiming with great voice: Who is worthy to open the scroll and loose its seals?
And no one in heaven nor upon earth nor under earth was able to open the scroll nor to look into it.
And I wept much because no one worthy was found to open the scroll nor to look into it.
And one of elders says to me: Do not weep. Look, the Lion from tribe of Judah, the Root of David, conquered, to open the scroll and its seven seals.

And I saw in midst of throne and four living creatures and in midst of elders a Lamb standing as having been slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of the Divine sent into all earth.
And he came and took from the right hand of the One sitting upon throne.
And when he took the scroll, the four living creatures and twenty-four elders fell before the Lamb, each having harp and golden bowls full of incense, which are prayers of holy ones.

And they sing new song, saying: Worthy are you to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain and by your blood/life purchased for the Divine from every tribe and tongue and people and nation,
and made them to our Divine a reign and priests, and they will reign upon earth.
And I saw and heard voice of many messengers around throne and living creatures and elders, and their number was myriads of myriads and thousands of thousands,
saying with great voice: Worthy is the Lamb who was slain to receive power and wealth and wisdom and strength and honor and glory and blessing.

And every creature in heaven and upon earth and under earth and upon sea, and all things in them, I heard saying: To the One sitting upon throne and to the Lamb be blessing and honor and glory and might into the ages of ages.
And the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell and worshiped.

translation_notes: Lion is revealed as slain-standing Lamb: Revelation's central power-reversal.
Blood/life purchases a multi-people priestly reign; no ethnic erasure.
The prayers of holy ones appear as incense before the seals open.
safety_note: The Lamb conquers through faithful self-giving witness, not domination. This should govern the book's later images.
